changes: OpenClaw shipped v2026.4.5 on 2026-04-06, which is the clearest fresh category-anchor signal in this run.
SwarmClaw followed its 2026-04-04 burst with v1.4.5 on 2026-04-05, reinforcing that the orchestration / control-plane lane is still iterating quickly.

PicoClaw kept the edge lane active with a fresh nightly on 2026-04-05, while Poco v0.4.0 and AstrBot v4.22.3 remained the most recent product-layer and adjacent-platform releases from the prior 48 hours.
Hermes Agent did not ship a newer release after v2026.4.3, but the canonical repo stayed active in the 2026-04-06 snapshot.
The canonical repo references are now cleaner in the dataset too: TinyClaw is tracked through TinyAGI and HiClaw through agentscope-ai/HiClaw.
whyItMatters: The important change today is that the reference implementation itself shipped again: this is not just derivative churn.
SwarmClaw still matters because repeated fast releases suggest sustained demand for management layers above a single assistant runtime.
Poco and AstrBot still support the same strategic read: productized sandbox UX and messaging-native platform overlap remain live pressure points around the category.
Updating TinyAGI and HiClaw canonical repo links matters for the hosted atlas because it keeps the watch grounded in the right official surfaces, not stale aliases.
This is still incremental movement inside the current map, so the taxonomy and executive read remain stable.
